season_info.title: Season Information
season_info.description: Tea performs best in cool humid hill conditions with frequent nutrient application and pruning.

location_note.title: Local Variation Note
location_note.message: Perennial plantation crops respond well to regular organic matter and split nutrient application.

fertilizer_section.title: Recommended Inputs
organic: Compost or farmyard manure 10–20 kg per plant
micronutrients: Magnesium or boron if deficiency symptoms appear

season_advice.title: Season Guidance
season_advice.rainy_season: Keep basins well drained and avoid nutrient runoff.
season_advice.dry_season: Irrigate regularly and mulch the basin to conserve moisture.
season_advice.winter_season: Continue normal management in frost-free regions.

application_section.title: Application Method
application_section.frequency: Multiple split doses across the year

water_management.title: Water Management
tips: Avoid prolonged water stagnation near roots

pest_disease.title: Pest & Disease Information
prevention: Maintain shade or mulch where needed

warnings.title: Important Warnings
points: Avoid placing fertilizers too close to the stem

events.activity: Split nutrient application and plant training
